evidence_text: GitHub reported four incidents in March 2026 that caused degraded performance across GitHub services.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, overview
source_excerpt: "In March, we experienced four incidents that resulted in degraded performance across GitHub services."
note:
This is the source's top-level incident count for the month.
```
caveat:
This is the source's top-level incident count for the month.
```

evidence_text: On March 3, 2026, GitHub had degraded availability affecting github.com, the GitHub API, GitHub Actions, Git operations, GitHub Copilot, and other dependent services.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 incident
source_excerpt: "On March 3, 2026, between 18:46 and 20:09 UTC, GitHub experienced a period of degraded availability impacting github.com, the GitHub API, GitHub Actions, Git operations, GitHub Copilot, and other dependent services."
note:
The report section header lists the incident as "March 03 18:59 UTC (lasting 1 hour and 10 minutes)," while the body gives the impact window as 18:46 to 20:09 UTC.
```
caveat:
The report section header lists the incident as "March 03 18:59 UTC (lasting 1 hour and 10 minutes)," while the body gives the impact window as 18:46 to 20:09 UTC.
```

evidence_text: At the March 3 incident peak, github.com request failures were approximately 40% and GitHub API request failures were approximately 43%.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 request failures
source_excerpt: "At the peak of the incident, github.com request failures reached approximately 40%. During the same period, approximately 43% of GitHub API requests failed."
note:
These are peak-period service-specific failure rates stated by the source.
```
caveat:
These are peak-period service-specific failure rates stated by the source.
```

evidence_text: During the March 3 incident, Git operations over HTTP had an error rate of about 6%, SSH was not impacted, and GitHub Copilot requests had an error rate of about 21%.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 git and Copilot impact
source_excerpt: "Git operations over HTTP had an error rate of approximately 6%, while SSH was not impacted. GitHub Copilot requests had an error rate of approximately 21%."
note:
This unit groups related service-specific impact metrics from the same incident passage.
```
caveat:
This unit groups related service-specific impact metrics from the same incident passage.
```

evidence_text: GitHub Actions experienced less than 1% impact during the March 3 incident.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 Actions impact
source_excerpt: "GitHub Actions experienced less than 1% impact."
note:
This is the source's stated Actions impact level for the March 3 incident.
```
caveat:
This is the source's stated Actions impact level for the March 3 incident.
```

evidence_text: GitHub said the March 3 incident had the same underlying cause as an early-February incident involving a large volume of writes to the user settings caching mechanism.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 underlying cause linkage
source_excerpt: "This incident shared the same underlying cause as an incident in early February, where we saw a large volume of writes to the user settings caching mechanism."
note:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because the report links this incident to a prior failure mode rather than fully detailing the entire architecture.
```
caveat:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because the report links this incident to a prior failure mode rather than fully detailing the entire architecture.
```

evidence_text: A deployment intended to reduce write burden instead triggered every user's cache to expire, be recalculated, and be rewritten.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 deployment bug
source_excerpt: "While deploying a change to reduce the burden of these writes, a bug caused every user’s cache to expire, get recalculated, and get rewritten."
note:
This is the source's direct causal description of the triggering bug.
```
caveat:
This is the source's direct causal description of the triggering bug.
```

evidence_text: GitHub attributed the March 3 service impact to increased load that caused replication delays which then cascaded to affected services.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 cascading load effect
source_excerpt: "The increased load caused replication delays that cascaded down to all affected services."
note:
This directly states the mechanism connecting the cache event to broader service degradation.
```
caveat:
This directly states the mechanism connecting the cache event to broader service degradation.
```

evidence_text: GitHub mitigated the March 3 incident by rolling back the faulty deployment, then said it added a killswitch and improved monitoring for the caching mechanism and planned to move that mechanism to a dedicated host.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 3 mitigation and follow-up
source_excerpt:
"We mitigated this issue by immediately rolling back the faulty deployment."

"We have added a killswitch and improved monitoring to the caching mechanism to ensure we are notified before there is user impact and can respond swiftly."

"We are moving the cache mechanism to a dedicated host, ensuring that any future issues will solely affect services that rely on it."
note:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because these are mitigation and containment steps rather than a complete account of system behavior.
```
caveat:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because these are mitigation and containment steps rather than a complete account of system behavior.
```

evidence_text: On March 5, 2026, GitHub Actions was degraded between 16:24 and 19:30 UTC.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 Actions degradation
source_excerpt: "On March 5, 2026, between 16:24 and 19:30 UTC, GitHub Actions was degraded."
note:
The section header summarizes this incident as "March 05 16:35 UTC (lasting 2 hours and 55 minutes)."
```
caveat:
The section header summarizes this incident as "March 05 16:35 UTC (lasting 2 hours and 55 minutes)."
```

evidence_text: During the March 5 incident, 95% of workflow runs failed to start within 5 minutes and the average delay was 30 minutes.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 workflow delays
source_excerpt: "During this time, 95% of workflow runs failed to start within 5 minutes with an average delay of 30 minutes"
note:
This unit preserves the stated startup-delay metric for Actions workflows.
```
caveat:
This unit preserves the stated startup-delay metric for Actions workflows.
```

evidence_text: During the March 5 incident, 10% of workflow runs failed with an infrastructure error.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 infrastructure errors
source_excerpt: "and 10% of workflow runs failed with an infrastructure error."
note:
This is a separate failure metric from the startup-delay metric.
```
caveat:
This is a separate failure metric from the startup-delay metric.
```

evidence_text: GitHub attributed the March 5 Actions incident to Redis resiliency updates that introduced incorrect configuration changes in a Redis load balancer, routing internal traffic to an incorrect host.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 Redis load balancer cause
source_excerpt: "This was due to Redis infrastructure updates that were being rolled out to production to improve our resiliency. These updates introduced a set of incorrect configuration changes into our Redis load balancer, causing internal traffic to be routed to an incorrect host leading to two incidents."
note:
This is the source's direct cause statement for the incident.
```
caveat:
This is the source's direct cause statement for the incident.
```

evidence_text: GitHub said it mitigated the March 5 incident by correcting the misconfigured load balancer; Actions jobs were running successfully starting at 17:24 UTC, and the remaining incident time was spent draining the queued jobs.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 mitigation and queue recovery
source_excerpt: "We mitigated this incident by correcting the misconfigured load balancer. Actions jobs were running successfully starting at 17:24 UTC. The remaining time until we closed the incident was spent burning through the queue of jobs."
note:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because this describes mitigation and recovery behavior rather than the underlying design in full.
```
caveat:
Mechanism relevance is bounded because this describes mitigation and recovery behavior rather than the underlying design in full.
```

evidence_text: After the March 5 incident, GitHub said it rolled back the contributing updates, froze further changes in that area, and planned automation, alerting, and Redis client configuration improvements to prevent recurrence and improve resiliency.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 5 follow-up controls
source_excerpt: "We immediately rolled back the updates that were a contributing factor and have frozen all changes in this area until we complete follow-up work. We are working to improve our automation to ensure incorrect configuration changes cannot propagate through our infrastructure. We are also working on improved alerting to catch misconfigured load balancers before it becomes an incident. Additionally, we are updating the Redis client configuration in Actions to improve resiliency to brief cache interruptions."
note:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the report says resiliency-oriented updates themselves introduced the misconfiguration, leaving a visible gap between intended and observed resilience.
```
caveat:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the report says resiliency-oriented updates themselves introduced the misconfiguration, leaving a visible gap between intended and observed resilience.
```

evidence_text: The Copilot Coding Agent service was degraded in two incidents on March 19 and March 20, 2026, during which users could not start new Copilot Agent sessions or view existing ones.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 19-20 Copilot Agent outages
source_excerpt: "On March 19, 2026, between 01:05 and 02:52 UTC, and again on March 20, 2026, between 00:42 and 01:58 UTC, the Copilot Coding Agent service was degraded and users were unable to start new Copilot Agent sessions or view existing ones."
note:
This unit captures the service impact and the two incident windows together.
```
caveat:
This unit captures the service impact and the two incident windows together.
```

evidence_text: In the first Copilot Coding Agent incident, the average error rate was about 53% and peaked at about 93%; in the second, the average error rate was about 99% and peaked at about 100%, with significant retry amplification.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 19-20 Copilot Agent error rates
source_excerpt: "During the first incident, the average error rate was ~53% and peaked at ~93% of requests to the service. During the second incident, the average error rate was ~99% and peaked at ~100% of requests with significant retry amplification."
note:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the second occurrence was materially worse and included retry amplification, which the source presents as part of the unresolved incident behavior.
```
caveat:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the second occurrence was materially worse and included retry amplification, which the source presents as part of the unresolved incident behavior.
```

evidence_text: GitHub said both Copilot Coding Agent incidents were caused by a system authentication issue that prevented the service from connecting to its backing datastore.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 19-20 datastore auth cause
source_excerpt: "Both incidents were caused by the same underlying system authentication issue that prevented the service from connecting to its backing datastore."
note:
This is the source's direct cause statement for both incidents.
```
caveat:
This is the source's direct cause statement for both incidents.
```

evidence_text: GitHub said it mitigated each Copilot Coding Agent incident by rotating affected credentials, and it stated the second incident happened because remediation after the first incident was incomplete.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 19-20 mitigation and incomplete remediation
source_excerpt: "We mitigated each incident by rotating the affected credentials, which restored connectivity and returned error rates to normal. The mitigation time was 01:24. The second occurrence was due to an incomplete remediation of the first."
note:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the source explicitly says the same problem recurred due to incomplete remediation after the first incident.
```
caveat:
Contradiction relevance is tension because the source explicitly says the same problem recurred due to incomplete remediation after the first incident.
```

evidence_text: On March 24, 2026, GitHub's Microsoft Teams Integration and Teams Copilot Integration were unable to reliably deliver GitHub event notifications to Microsoft Teams; the average error rate was 37.4%, the peak was 90.1%, about 19% of installs failed to receive notifications during the period, and GitHub attributed the incident to an upstream dependency outage causing HTTP 500 errors and connection resets.
citation: GitHub availability report: March 2026, March 24 Teams integration outage
source_excerpt:
"On March 24, 2026, between 15:57 and 19:51 UTC, the Microsoft Teams Integration and Teams Copilot Integration services were degraded and unable to deliver GitHub event notifications to Microsoft Teams. On average, the error rate was 37.4% and peaked at 90.1% of requests to the service—approximately 19% of all integration installs failed to receive GitHub-to-Teams notifications in this time period."

"This was due to an outage at one of our upstream dependencies, which caused HTTP 500 errors and connection resets for our Teams integration."
note: Mechanism relevance is bounded because the report attributes the incident to an upstream dependency outage without detailing that dependency's internal failure mode.
caveat: Mechanism relevance is bounded because the report attributes the incident to an upstream dependency outage without detailing that dependency's internal failure mode.
